text:
Credit Card Underwriting Policy
1. Purpose
This policy defines eligibility and underwriting standards for issuing unsecured 
credit cards to retail customers.
2. Customer Eligibility
Applicants must:
Be between 21 and 65 years of age.
Be a resident of the country.
Possess a valid government-issued identification document.
Provide proof of income.
3. Credit Score Requirements
 Credit Score Decision
 750 and above Preferred Approval
 700 – 749 Standard Approval
 650 – 699 Manual Review Required
 Below 650 Decline
4. Income Requirements
 Customer Type Minimum Annual Income
 Salaried ₹400,000
 Self-Employed ₹600,000
Income must be supported by appropriate documentation.
5. Credit Utilization Guidelines
 Utilization Ratio Risk Assessment
 Below 30% Low Risk
 30% - 50% Moderate Risk
 Above 50% Elevated Risk
 Above 75% High Risk Review
6. Debt-to-Income (DTI) Ratio
Maximum acceptable DTI ratio:
Standard applicants: 40%
Premium applicants: 35%
Applications exceeding these thresholds require manual review.
7. Automatic Decline Criteria
Applications must be declined when:
Credit score is below 650.
Bankruptcy is reported within the last 5 years.
Fraud investigation is active.
Income verification cannot be completed.
DTI exceeds 60%.
8. Approval Decision Framework
Approval decisions should consider:
Credit score
Income stability
Credit utilization
Existing obligations
Payment history
No single factor should be used in isolation.

Personal Loan Credit Policy
## 1. Purpose
This policy governs underwriting decisions for unsecured personal loans.
---
## 2. Applicant Eligibility
Applicants must:
* Be at least 21 years old.
* Have stable employment history of at least 12 months.
* Maintain an active bank account.
* Meet minimum income requirements.
---
## 3. Credit Score Evaluation
| Credit Score | Lending Decision |
| ------------ | ---------------- |
| Above 760    | Excellent        |
| 700 - 759    | Good             |
| 650 - 699    | Acceptable       |
| Below 650    | High Risk        |
Applications below 650 generally require rejection.
---
## 4. Income Requirements
Minimum annual income:
* Salaried applicants: ₹500,000
* Self-employed applicants: ₹750,000
---
## 5. Employment Stability
Preferred employment tenure:
* Minimum 12 months with current employer.
* Minimum 24 months business operation for self-employed applicants.
---
## 6. Loan-to-Income Assessment
Recommended loan amount:
* Not more than 10 times monthly income.
Applications exceeding this threshold require enhanced review.
---
## 7. Risk Indicators
The following increase risk:
* Multiple recent credit inquiries.
* Significant recent increase in debt.
* High revolving credit utilization.
* Irregular repayment history.
---
## 8. Decline Conditions
Applications should be declined when:
* Income verification fails.
* Fraud indicators are confirmed.
* Active bankruptcy exists.
* Credit score is below policy threshold.

Premium Credit Card Eligibility Policy
## 1. Purpose
This policy defines eligibility requirements for premium and rewards-based credit 
card products.
---
## 2. Target Customer Segment
Premium credit cards are intended for:
* High-income professionals.
* Business owners.
* Affluent retail customers.
* Existing customers with strong repayment history.
---
## 3. Minimum Eligibility Criteria
Applicants must satisfy all of the following:
* Age between 25 and 65 years.
* Credit score of at least 750.
* Annual income of at least ₹1,500,000.
* No delinquency within the previous 24 months.
---
## 4. Preferred Customer Characteristics
Preference may be given to customers who:
* Maintain utilization below 30%.
* Hold existing banking relationships.
* Demonstrate stable employment.
* Maintain low debt obligations.
---
## 5. Credit Limit Assignment
| Credit Score | Indicative Credit Limit |
| ------------ | ----------------------- |
| 750 - 799    | ₹300,000 - ₹500,000     |
| 800 - 849    | ₹500,000 - ₹1,000,000   |
| 850+         | Above ₹1,000,000        |
Final limits remain subject to underwriting review.
---
## 6. Review Triggers
Applications require manual review when:
* Utilization exceeds 50%.
* Income documentation is incomplete.
* DTI exceeds 40%.
* Credit history is less than 24 months.
---
## 7. Automatic Rejection Criteria
Applications must be rejected when:
* Credit score is below 750.
* Fraud alerts are present.
* Bankruptcy is reported.
* Material misrepresentation is identified.
---
## 8. Approval Principles
Premium card approvals should prioritize:
* Strong repayment history.
* Sustainable income.
* Low leverage.
* Long-term customer value.
